steps.title: Identify the grammar rule at stake
steps.content: The blank follows an opening participial phrase: “Fortifying soil in arid ecosystems against erosion, ______”. A participial phrase must immediately precede the noun it modifies — so the subject of the main clause must be the thing doing the fortifying. Biocrusts fortify soil, so the subject must be “biocrusts” or a synonym like “these crusts.”

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• "a recent study's estimate is that these crusts reduce global dust emissions by 60 percent each year.": The subject is “a recent study’s estimate,” not biocrusts — a study estimate does not fortify soil. ✗
  • "an estimated 60 percent reduction in global dust emissions each year is due to these crusts, according to a recent study.": The subject is “an estimated 60 percent reduction,” which cannot fortify soil. ✗
  • "these crusts reduce global dust emissions by an estimated 60 percent each year, according to a recent study.": The subject is “these crusts,” which logically fortifies soil — the participial phrase attaches correctly. ✓
  • "a recent study has estimated that these crusts reduce global dust emissions by 60 percent each year.": The subject is “a recent study,” which cannot fortify soil. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
